messages.content: Mission target: 192.168.56.101. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: This is the start of the engagement. Target IP 192.168.56.101 is confirmed. No prior reconnaissance data exists — I have zero information about this target. No running agents, no prior findings. Mission objective: find flag.txt. HYPOTHESIS: Without a scan, I cannot form any attack hypothesis. The target could be Windows, Linux, a network device, or something else entirely. It could be running vsftpd 2.3.4 (instant root), or it could be a patched modern system. I literally do not know. OPTIONS: A) Spawn a scanner agent — standard PTES first step, reveals all open ports and service versions before any exploitation. B) Spawn an osint agent — useless without a domain or organization name, and this is an internal IP. C) Try to spawn exploit agents blind — reckless, violates enumeration-before-exploitation principle, and I don't have port numbers to use in task_type. DECISION: Option A. A comprehensive TCP+UDP port scan is mandatory before any exploitation. Port numbers in task_type MUST come from scan findings. I cannot invent or guess them. Spawn the scanner now; once results return, I will analyze ALL services simultaneously and spawn exploit agents for every viable vector in parallel.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "port_scan", "options": {"scan_type": "full", "tcp_ports": "1-65535", "udp_ports": "top_1000", "service_detection": true, "os_detection": true, "script_scan": true}}}
